data.title: NEAR Teases Major Announcement Scheduled for July 7
data.description:
NEAR said it will release an important announcement on July 7.

According to Foresight News, NEAR previewed the update in a post that included the phrase “for Business.”

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Cuba’s Shift Toward Free Markets Fails to Convince US
data.description: Cuba is struggling to persuade the administration of US President Donald Trump that its most ambitious economic reforms in decades are enough to drive the changes the US is demanding, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Bank of England’s Mann Says Some Sectors’ Labor Signals Are Not as Weak as Overall Jobless Rate Suggests
data.description:
Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee member Mann said labor market signals in some sectors were not as weak as the overall unemployment rate suggested.

According to Jin10, Mann said that in parts of the economy, labor market indicators did not appear as soft as what the headline jobless rate showed.

data.title: STOCKS | Tesla Q2 EV Deliveries Rise to 480,126, Beating StreetAccount Estimate
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) reported second-quarter electric vehicle deliveries of 480,126 units, above the StreetAccount analyst consensus estimate of 406,600, according to Ming Pao.

Tesla delivered about 384,000 vehicles in the same period a year earlier, while first-quarter deliveries this year were 358,000.

data.title: Bank Of England MPC Member Mann Says Short- And Long-Term Expectations Remain High
data.description:
Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee member Mann said short- and long-term expectations remained high.

According to Jin10, Mann made the remarks without providing additional details.

data.title: STOCKS | HSBC Raises Intel Price Target to $200 From $100
data.description: HSBC Holdings has raised its price target for Intel to $200 from $100. According to Odaily, the announcement was reported in a brief update.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Holds Talks With Samsung on Custom AI Chips, Report Says
data.description:
Anthropic is in talks with Samsung about developing customized artificial intelligence chips, The Information reported.

According to Jin10, the report said Anthropic has begun early-stage development work on its own AI chips.

data.title: Comcast Shares Slide Despite Planned NBCUniversal Spinoff
data.description:
Comcast (CMCSA) shares have continued to fall even as the communication services sector rises, with the stock trading around $23.73 near a 52-week low. According to BeInCrypto, Comcast’s planned tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky into two independent publicly traded companies failed to sustain a rally after the announcement.

The stock jumped about 19% to nearly $27 on June 29 but reversed to $23.73 by July 1, down 3.34% on the session. BeInCrypto cited broadband customer losses to fixed wireless rivals Verizon and T-Mobile, and noted NBCUniversal’s Peacock lost $432 million last quarter.

data.title: CACEIS Holds Exclusive Talks to Acquire French Crypto Platform Meria
data.description:
CACEIS is in exclusive talks to acquire French crypto investment platform Meria.

According to NS3.AI, Meria serves 150,000 users and manages approximately €350 million in assets under management.

Meria holds a MiCA crypto-asset service provider (CASP) license and offers crypto brokerage and staking products.

data.title: White House NEC Director Hassett Says It Is Unusual Powell Has Not Left the Fed
data.description:
White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said it is highly unusual that Jerome Powell has not yet left the Federal Reserve.

According to Odaily, Hassett also said he is concerned about Powell’s motivations for remaining at the Fed.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | U.S.-Listed Precious Metals Miners Rise, With Gold Fields Up 5.6%
data.description:
Shares of U.S.-listed precious metals mining companies rose, led by Gold Fields, which gained 5.6%. According to Jin10, Harmony Gold rose 7%, Newmont Mining gained 3.8%, and Barrick Gold advanced 4.1%.

The SPDR Gold ETF rose 2.3%.

data.title: Aave Co-Founder Stani Kulechov Says Aave DAO Swap Revenue Reaches $1 Million for First Time
data.description:
Aave DAO’s swap revenue has reached $1 million for the first time since the launch of the “Aave Will Win” initiative, according to a statement by Aave co-founder Stani Kulechov.

According to Foresight News, Kulechov said the revenue stream adds transaction-based income to the Aave protocol alongside its existing fees tied to assets under management (AUM).

He added that Aave plans to introduce additional features in its Aave Pro product, including leverage tools, a transaction builder, and support for Aave V4, with the aim of expanding revenue channels.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 1,700 USDT with a 7.98% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 02, 2026, 13:35 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 1,700 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 1,703.209961 USDT, with a narrowed 7.98% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Indexes Open Higher on July 2
data.description:
U.S. stock indexes opened higher on July 2 (Thursday).

According to Jin10, the Dow Jones Industrial Average opened up 267.70 points, or 0.51%, at 52,572.94. The S&P 500 opened up 24.61 points, or 0.33%, at 7,507.84. The Nasdaq Composite opened up 47.62 points, or 0.18%, at 26,087.65.

data.title: White House NEC Director Hassett Says Jobs Data Align With Strong Economy, Backs Fed Independence
data.description:
White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said U.S. employment data are consistent with a strong economy and reiterated respect for the Federal Reserve’s independence. According to Odaily, Hassett said there is no reason to believe economic growth will lead to inflation.

He also said he believes Chair of the Federal Reserve Kevin Warsh can persuade his colleagues to accept his views.

data.title: UK Treasury Roadmap Calls for Tokenization and Digital Money in Future Retail Payments
data.description:
UK regulators are urging tokenization and “new forms of digital money” to become part of the core infrastructure for the country’s future retail payment ecosystem. According to Cointelegraph, HM Treasury, on behalf of the Payments Vision Delivery Committee, said in a Thursday update to the government’s roadmap for modernizing retail payment systems that incorporating tokenization and digital money would support efforts to build a “diverse multi-money ecosystem.” The update also highlighted “programmable payments, including those that rely on tokenization,” as potential “product-level arrangements” that could help drive payment innovation in the UK. It further called for infrastructure that allows emerging forms of digital money to interact with traditional payment systems, reflecting an expansion of the National Payments Vision document published in November.

The policy direction comes alongside broader regulatory and operational initiatives affecting crypto and tokenized finance. Earlier this week, the UK’s Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) published its crypto regulatory framework and said the licensing window for crypto companies will open from September until Feb. 28, 2027, ahead of the regime going live on Oct. 25, 2027. Under the framework, cryptocurrency firms—including trading platforms, custodians, stablecoin issuers, staking companies, and other intermediaries—would need FCA authorization to operate in the UK. Separately, in April, the UK government said it would revisit its payments rulebook to support adoption of new payment technologies, including stablecoins and tokenization, and outlined plans for a consultation on reforms to payment services and electronic money rules to create a single framework for traditional and tokenized payments, including stablecoins and tokenized deposits, in an April 21 announcement by HM Treasury and Economic Secretary to the Treasury Lucy Rigby. In the following month, the Bank of England proposed extending operating hours for its core settlement infrastructure toward near-24/7 availability, citing support for cross-border payments and new payment and settlement models as tokenization develops; it is seeking public feedback until July 3 and plans to publish a feedback statement in the summer. The FCA also said days earlier that tokenization and distributed ledger technologies could improve fund management efficiency and support innovation in the UK asset management sector.

data.title: Anchorage Digital Integrates Lido to Offer Institutional Access to wstETH
data.description:
Crypto bank Anchorage Digital said it has integrated Lido, Ethereum’s largest liquid staking protocol, to provide institutional clients with direct access to the derivative asset wstETH. According to ChainCatcher, institutional users can mint and redeem wstETH through Anchorage while earning Ethereum staking yields.

Anchorage said the offering combines access to wstETH with its custody services and a governance and compliance framework for institutional clients.

data.title: Tesla Shares Trim Premarket Gains After Q2 Deliveries Beat Estimates
data.description: Tesla shares rose more than 2% in premarket trading before paring gains to under 1% after the company reported its second-quarter delivery results. According to ChainCatcher, citing a Jin10 report, Tesla posted total Q2 deliveries of 480,126 vehicles, exceeding market expectations.

data.title: Coinglass Data Show Hyperliquid Whale Holds $4.69B in Positions, Slightly Net Short
data.description:
A whale on Hyperliquid is holding $4.69 billion in total positions, with short exposure slightly exceeding longs, according to Coinglass data. According to ChainCatcher, the whale’s long positions total $2.28 billion, representing 48.48% of holdings, while short positions total $2.42 billion, representing 51.52%.

The data show the whale’s profit and loss stands at -$96.28 million on long positions and -$21.00 million on short positions.

ChainCatcher also reported that a whale address, 0x50b3..20, opened a 23x cross-margin short position on ETH at a price of $1,541.36. The position currently shows an unrealized loss of -$6.63 million.

data.title: Eric Balchunas Says June ETF Market Set Near-Record Issuance, Trading Volume, and Inflows
data.description:
Bloomberg senior ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said the ETF market delivered a “JUNE-SANITY” level performance in June, with multiple indicators nearing or setting records.

According to Odaily, data showed 214 new ETF products were launched in June, or about 10 per day, marking a record high. ETF trading turnover reached $7 trillion, the second-highest level on record.

The report also said net ETF inflows totaled $191 billion in June, the second-highest monthly level on record, averaging about $9 billion per day across roughly 2,700 different funds.

Balchunas said the figures indicate broad strength in ETF inflows, new product issuance, and trading activity.
